div.head: 86. Editorial Note
div.p: On January 28, 1961, President Kennedy nominated Henry R. Labouisse to succeed James W. Riddleberger as Director of the International Cooperation Administration (ICA). Labouisse, a lawyer, held various appointments with the U.S. Government from 1941 to 1951. He served with the Mutual Security Agency 1951-1954 and was Director of the U.N. Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees 1954-1958. Labouisse’s nomination as ICA Director was approved by the Senate on February 22, and he was sworn in on March 1.
